description:
Salmon (version 1.6.0) transcriptome indexer.
See\: The Salmon User Guide for detailed usage guidelines.
author: Anthony S. Castanza;UCSD-MesirovLab
plan:
## Salmon.Indexer GenePattern Module — Implementation Plan ### 1. Overview Salmon.Indexer is a GenePattern module that Salmon (version 1.6.0) transcriptome indexer.
\n\nSee\: The Salmon User Guide\nfor detailed usage guidelines. ### 2. Parameters The module exposes 6 parameters: - Required: Index.Mode, Use.Gencode - Optional: GTF.gz, Transcriptome.fa.gz, Genome.fa.gz, kmer ### 3. Command Line ``` salmon_index.sh -a -t -g -k -m -f -l -j ``` ### 4. Docker Image Docker image: genepattern/salmon-indexer\:beta_1.6.0 ### 5. Validation Strategy - Validate all required parameters are provided - Validate file formats match expected extensions - Check parameter value ranges where applicable ### 6. Implementation Roadmap Phase 1: Wrapper script implementation Phase 2: Docker container setup Phase 3: Parameter validation and error handling Phase 4: Testing and documentation

parameters.name: GTF.gz
parameters.description: GTF file (must be gzipped).\nOnly used if a transcriptome fasta is not provided to the 'Transcriptome fa gz' parameter, or producing a decoy-aware index. Both require that a genome fasta is provided to the 'Genome fa gz' parameter.

parameters.name: Transcriptome.fa.gz
parameters.description:
Transcriptome FASTA file (must be gzipped)
\nIf no Transcriptome FASTA is provided the module will attempt to generate one from the GTF and Genomic FASTA using gffread. This procedure has not been fully tested.\n

parameters.name: Genome.fa.gz
parameters.description: Genomic FASTA file (must be gzipped). Used when producing a decoy-aware index, or when reconstructing a transcriptome from a provided gtf.

parameters.name: kmer
parameters.description: kmer size used for indexing (must be odd)

parameters.name: Index.Mode
parameters.description:
There are two options for generating a decoy-aware transcriptome\:
Full\: Uses the entire genome of the organism as the decoy sequence. This scheme provides a more comprehensive set of decoys resulting in more accurate quantification, but, produces a substantially larger index, requires considerably more memory to build and quantify against the index, and quantifications against the index are slower.
Partial\: Computes a set of decoy sequences by mapping the annotated transcripts you wish to index against a hard-masked version of the organism's genome. This produces a smaller index and requires substantially less memory quantify against, but may be more susceptible to spurious mappings.
If no Genome fasta is provided, this parameter will be ignored.

parameters.name: Use.Gencode
parameters.description: Use Gencode format parsing for Transcriptome fasta record IDs
